assertions.description: navigator.permissions is the standard surface for querying permission state — the entry point this intervention applies to.
navigator.permissions.query for a known descriptor returns a PermissionStatus whose state is granted | denied | prompt.
Sensitive permission prompts (e.g. geolocation) called without user activation are not auto-granted — they resolve to a non-granted state or reject, never silently allow.
iframe elements expose the allow attribute used to grant permissions selectively to embedded documents.
document.featurePolicy (or document.permissionsPolicy) remains the introspection surface for the active policy.
PermissionStatus exposes onchange so pages can react to intervention-driven revocations.
